title: Customer
description:
A business or individual the tenant has any relationship with — leads, prospects, active customers, churned. The CRM-style entity. Contracts, billing terms, and SLAs live on a separate service_contract entity that links here (a customer can have many contracts over time; at most one is active at a time).

v2 (2026-05-20): Door C split — the contract-side fields (CPI, payment terms, SLAs, hourly rates, contract_template) moved out to service_contract.schema.json. Identification fields (ABN/ACN/legal name) stay here because they're properties of the entity, not the contract.

customer_id.description: Internal canonical ID. Generated on import if not provided.

external_id.description: Customer's own ID in an external system (SimPRO CustomerID, MYOB CardID, etc.). Preserved for round-trip exports.

type.description: Lifecycle classification. lead = early interest, no contact yet. prospect = engaged, quote in flight. active = under contract (has at least one service_contract with status=active). churned = was a customer, contract ended.

company_name.description: Business / company name. Either company_name OR (first_name + last_name) must be set (cross-field rule customer_has_a_name).

first_name.description: Given name for individual customers (sole traders, residential).

display_name.description: Optional override for how the customer is shown in UI / reports. Falls back to company_name or full personal name.

code.description: Short identifier used in schedules and reports (e.g. 'EQX-NAT', 'EQX-HS', 'RHC').

customer_entity_legal_name.description: Full legal entity name on registration documents (may differ from display name). Property of the entity, not any specific contract.

customer_entity_abn.description: Australian Business Number (11 digits, stored as string to preserve formatting).

customer_entity_acn.description: Australian Company Number (9 digits, stored as string).

phone.description: Primary phone. Coerced to AU format where possible.

address.description: Free-text head office / billing address. Site-level addresses live on sites.

first_engaged_at.description: Date this customer entered the CRM (lead create date).

became_active_at.description: Date the customer became active (first contract signed). Null for leads / prospects.

churned_at.description: Date the customer churned. Null while active.

active.description: Whether this customer record is currently active (i.e. not soft-deleted). Distinct from `type=active` which is a lifecycle stage.

x-eq-cross-field-rules.rule: (company_name != null AND company_name != '') OR (first_name != null AND first_name != '') OR (last_name != null AND last_name != '')
x-eq-cross-field-rules.message: Customer must have either a company_name or a personal name (first_name / last_name).
Customer is marked active but has no became_active_at date — should align with their first contract's term_start.
Customer is marked churned but has no churned_at date.

x-eq-import-hints.description: Customer registers are usually small (10-100 rows) and come from accounting exports, CRM exports, or a manual list. The lifecycle stage often needs to be inferred from whether the customer has any contracts.

common_sources.name: Accounting export (Xero / MYOB customer list)
watchouts: ABN may have spaces or no formatting — preserve as text
Some rows are individuals — populate first_name/last_name not company_name

common_sources.name: SimPRO customer export
watchouts: SimPRO 'Type' may not map 1:1 to our lifecycle enum — use x-eq-enum-aliases on `type`

common_sources.name: CRM pipeline export
watchouts: Stage maps to `type` via aliases (e.g. 'Qualified' → prospect)
